content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the milestone lifecycle implementation in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs` and `src/cli/run.rs`. I found two correctness gaps: successful late completions can remain stuck in `paused`, and failed project runs still never become milestone `failed` on the normal sync path. I also ran `cargo test paused_milestone_stays_paused_when_completion_reconciles_lineage --lib` and `cargo test sync_terminal_milestone_task_leaves_failed_runs_paused_for_resume --lib`; both pass today because the incorrect behaviors are currently baked into the code and tests.

## Amendments

1. Fix the normal project-run sync path so failed runs drive a real milestone `failed` transition instead of parking the milestone in `paused`. `src/cli/run.rs:181-194` still maps `RunStatus::Failed` to `CompletionMilestoneDisposition::ReconcileFromLineage`, and the downstream reconciliation in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:983-995` only downgrades an idle running milestone to `Paused`. The current behavior is codified by `sync_terminal_milestone_task_leaves_failed_runs_paused_for_resume` in `src/cli/run.rs:838-926`, which asserts `MilestoneStatus::Paused` after a durable `run_failed` event. That leaves the promised `running -> failed` lifecycle transition unreachable on the main sync path and suppresses the required failed journal event.
   Rationale: This is a direct contract violation for unrecoverable failures on the production path: the system records a retryable pause where the bead requires a terminal milestone failure.
2. Fix paused-terminal reconciliation so a milestone whose last in-flight bead finishes successfully can reach `completed` instead of remaining stuck in `paused`. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:983-991`, the `snapshot.status == MilestoneStatus::Paused` branch short-circuits before the completed-state check, so `record_bead_completion` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2073-2123`) preserves `Paused` even when `completed_beads + skipped_beads >= total_beads` and no bead is active. The regression is locked in by `paused_milestone_stays_paused_when_completion_reconciles_lineage` at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5326-5393`. A fully closed milestone should synthesize the allowed bridge and end in `completed`, otherwise the completion journal transition is never emitted.
   Rationale: This leaves semantically finished milestones in a resumable non-terminal state and breaks the bead’s core requirement to track milestones through completion.
